entries.title: On Hebrews 1:3
entries.excerpt: Who being the brightness of His Glory and the express Image of His person, and upholding all things by the word of His power, when He had by Himself purged our sins. Everywhere indeed a reverential mind is requisite, but especially when we say or hear anything of God: Since neither can tongue speak nor thought hear anything suitable to our God. And why speak I of tongue or thought? For not even the understanding which far excels these, will be able to comprehend anything accurately, when we desire to utter anything concerning God. For if the peace of God surpasses all understanding…

entries.title: On Hebrews 1:3
entries.excerpt: Hilary of Poitier, in the Word of God Who, according to Hebrews 1:3, is "the express I mage of God.
